title: Tri-View Layered Manipulation World Model
description: Dual-wrist + D435 data now; fixed-exo capture/synthesis target + object/contact layers
summary: Post-Gamma-World, this project is reframed away from generic synchronized multi-agent video generation. The current data reality is stricter than the first tri-view plan: Yuhang/Daimon UMI data has dual wrist fisheye views and a D435 main view, but no fixed third-person/exo view. The core question is therefore which view and which explicit layer carries manipulation-useful state, and whether fixed-exo evidence must be newly captured or can be synthesized well enough for reward/eval and failure analysis.
asset_alt: UMI dataset annotation and subtask segmentation visualization panel
asset_caption: Dataset QA: synchronized wrist views, 3D pose, EEF traces, current segment, and action log after subtask splitting.
details: Reorganized title / intro 2026-05-31: the project is now Tri-View Layered Manipulation World Model. UMI remains the main data and v0 training substrate, but the paper story is not UMI-only and not Image-Layered-only.

Gamma-World boundary: Gamma already occupies the generic synchronized action-conditioned multi-agent world-model lane with agent identity encoding, Sparse Hub Attention, and streaming rollout. Our contribution must avoid “dual-wrist video generation” as the main claim.

Core hypothesis: for tabletop manipulation, useful information is not “more cameras” by default. The key is whether fixed exo, wrist/ego, and explicit layers expose object state, contact, reward, and failure evidence better than flat generated pixels.

View design after data check: the available UMI substrate is left/right wrist fisheye plus a D435 main view. Fixed third-person/exo remains the desired reward/eval view, but it is not present in Yuhang/Daimon UMI data and must come from new capture or a risky cross-view synthesis probe.

Layer design: predict or infer scene/background, object/contact transition, robot/ego motion, human/occluder mask, and recomposed views. These layers should be state supervision for manipulation, not only editable visual PSD layers.

v0 baseline: keep Wan2.2-TI2V-5B LoRA step-125 as the dual-wrist concatenation baseline. It answers whether the current training run learned anything useful, but it is no longer the final contribution by itself.

v1 model direction: move from spatial concatenation toward structured view-axis conditioning and layer heads. Action should enter through explicit dual-arm pose/gripper traces, not only captions or first-frame appearance.

Evaluation direction: first compare wrist-only, D435-main, wrist+D435, and layer-head variants on existing data; only run fixed-exo-only / wrist+exo / wrist+exo+layers after new fixed-exo capture or a credible synthesized fixed view exists. Report visual quality only as secondary evidence; primary evidence should be object/contact consistency, action controllability, reward-model usefulness, failure attribution, and held-out task correlation.

Existing done evidence folded in: Qwen-Image-Layered proved scene/background separation is useful but robot/ego isolation is weak; object/contact and robot/ego probes justify explicit heads; WMFactory + iWorld-Bench gives the UI/eval harness; DreamX-World and Genie remain baselines/tools.

Data requirement update 2026-05-31: Yuhang/Daimon UMI data has no fixed third-person/exo view at all, not even non-fixed. Humanoid data with this setting is also very scarce. The usable existing format is two wrist fisheye cameras plus a D435 main view, so fixed-exo reward/eval either requires new synchronized capture or a high-risk EgoX/ego-exo generation feasibility probe.

Discussion update 2026-05-31: the immediate experiment is to test whether EgoX-style or broader ego/exo view translation can synthesize a stable fixed/main view from wrist fisheye + D435 evidence. This is not assumed to work; it is a go/no-go probe because the desired direction may be wrist/ego/main-D435 to fixed-exo, while EgoX itself is primarily exo-to-ego.

Positioning sentence for proposal: instead of asking whether synchronized robot videos can be generated, this project asks which views and explicit state layers make generated rollouts useful for manipulation reward, evaluation, and failure analysis.

references.title: SANA-WM: Efficient Minute-Scale World Modeling with Hybrid Linear Diffusion Transformer
references.notes: Reference for efficient minute-scale world modeling, hybrid linear attention, precise 6-DoF camera control, and metric pose-supervised long-video generation.

references.title: iWorld-Bench: A Benchmark for Interactive World Models with a Unified Action Generation Framework
references.notes: Reference for evaluating interactive/camera-controllable world models with packaged metadata, camera trajectories, action-control, memory-ability, camera-following, and all-metric evaluation wrappers.

references.title: Gamma-World: Generative Multi-Agent World Modeling Beyond Two Players
references.notes: Collision/baseline reference: generic synchronized action-conditioned multi-agent world model with Simplex Rotary Agent Encoding, Sparse Hub Attention, and streaming rollout. Use it to motivate our shift to view/layer-grounded manipulation evaluation.

references.title: EgoX: Egocentric Video Generation from a Single Exocentric Video
references.notes: Exo-to-ego reference: point-cloud rendered ego prior, width/channel latent concatenation, geometry-guided self-attention, LoRA on Wan 2.1 14B; useful for cross-view conditioning design.

references.title: Intention-driven Ego-to-Exo Video Generation
references.notes: Ego-to-exo reference: uses view-independent intention, head trajectory, action description, optical flow and occlusion maps to synthesize exocentric video from egocentric input.

references.title: WorldWander: Bridging Egocentric and Exocentric Worlds in Video Generation
references.notes: Bidirectional ego/exo translation reference with in-context perspective alignment and synchronized ego-exo triplets; relevant for tri-view cross-view synchronization.

references.title: Qwen-Image-Layered
references.notes: Layer-decomposition probe from the former Image-Layered card; useful for scene/background separation, but robot/ego and contact require explicit manipulation heads.

risks_decisions: Remote merge attempt 2026-05-31: Image-Layered code bundle is prepared locally from feibo; final installation on the UMI binding machine is blocked because jingxiang-b850m-c / 100.112.245.1 is offline on Tailscale.

Data reality 2026-05-31: Yuhang/Daimon UMI data has no fixed third-person/exo view, and humanoid data with this camera setup is extremely scarce. Do not assume fixed-exo reward/eval can be built from the old UMI data.
Available-view decision: treat two wrist fisheye cameras plus the D435 main view as the current train/eval substrate; fixed-exo is a target to synthesize or newly capture, not a guaranteed input.

EgoX-style fixed-view synthesis is a risky fallback: useful as a probe, but if camera stability, contact visibility, or object-state correctness is weak, the project must collect new synchronized fixed-exo data instead of building the paper claim on generated views.
Do not pitch the project as generic multi-agent synchronized world modeling; Gamma-World already occupies that lane.
Do not pitch the Wan2.2 dual-wrist concat LoRA as the final contribution; keep it as v0 baseline and qualitative/eval evidence.
The fixed-exo view is necessary for the new claim: without synchronized third-person data, reward/eval and global object-state arguments will be weak.
Layer heads must be manipulation-state heads, not aesthetic image-editing layers. Object/contact, robot/ego, occluder, and reward/eval utility are the criteria.
Any quality claim needs a comparison against base WAN, step-125 LoRA, GT, and a Gamma-style synchronized-agent framing where feasible.
If ego/wrist views do not improve over fixed-exo for tabletop tasks, that is still a valid contribution: the project should report view utility rather than assume ego is always useful.
Data/schema risk: tri-view capture requires camera sync, calibration, and consistent action traces; otherwise layer/eval metrics become hard to trust.
Compute risk: 14B-scale Wan2.2 remains a separate 8xA100/H100-class request; current project plan should not depend on it.
模型规模澄清：当前已经在 003 / L40S 上跑完的是 Wan2.2-TI2V-5B LoRA，不是 2B；如果后续要推进 Wan2.2 A14B / 14B，需要单独按 8xA100/H100 80GB 级别申请算力，8xL30 24GB 不作为主方案。
运维决策：WAN2.2 微调进入实际运行前，需要向老师明确算力需求、GPU 时间窗口和预算；2 号机服务器需要通过 2.5 英寸 SSD 扩充外存，避免训练数据、checkpoint 和 cache 卡在磁盘容量上。

Dashboard structure decision 2026-05-31: UMI World Model and Image-Layered World Model are merged into one visible card. The former Image-Layered project file remains only as an archived pointer to avoid losing history.

timeline.target_note: Paper target for the tri-view layered manipulation world model; schedule should be updated once the exact submission plan is fixed.

milestones.title: Merge Image-Layered WM into UMI tri-view layered world-model card
Tri-view data schema and layer/eval ablation spec ready
